lesson.title: IELTS English Podcast - Speaking Topic: Technology

videos.title: IELTS English Podcast - Speaking Topic: Technology

vocabularies.definition: The subjects that are taught in a school, college, etc., or the things that are studied in a particular subject.
examples: The school has a very broad curriculum.
The curriculum includes history, science, and mathematics.
vocabularies.contextSentence: this is a 40 topic curriculum.

vocabularies.definition: A time of great success, happiness, or prosperity.
examples: The company is enjoying a golden period of growth.
The 1920s were a golden period for jazz music.
vocabularies.contextSentence: But for me right now, this is a real golden period.

vocabularies.definition: A person who is enthusiastic about new technology.
examples: He is a true technophile, always eager to try the latest gadgets.
Technophiles often embrace technological advancements quickly.
vocabularies.contextSentence: The first group are called technophiles.

vocabularies.definition: A person who dislikes or fears new technology.
examples: My grandmother is a technophobe and struggles with computers.
Technophobes may avoid using new technologies due to fear or lack of understanding.
vocabularies.contextSentence: The second group are called technophobes.

vocabularies.definition: A small, useful machine or device.
examples: I have a lot of gadgets in my kitchen.
This new gadget can translate languages in real time.
vocabularies.contextSentence: What gadgets do you use on a daily basis?

vocabularies.definition: Without any delay; instantly.
examples: I knew immediately that something was wrong.
The doctor told me to call him immediately if the symptoms worsened.
vocabularies.contextSentence: The ones that come to my mind immediately are my phone, my laptop,

vocabularies.definition: To improve something or make it more modern.
examples: I decided to upgrade my computer.
The company is planning to upgrade its software.
vocabularies.contextSentence: but last year I decided to upgrade to kind of a top of the range.

vocabularies.definition: Using or based on intuition; readily learned or understood.
examples: The software has an intuitive interface.
The design is intuitive and easy to use.
vocabularies.contextSentence: You know, it's quite an intuitive design, so it's quite easy to learn how to use it,

vocabularies.definition: The process or art of making videos.
examples: He has a passion for videography.
She is studying videography at university.
vocabularies.contextSentence: because as I said, I do like videography, particularly, I want to take good videos of my daughter

vocabularies.definition: No longer produced or used; out of date.
examples: Many traditional farming methods have become obsolete.
The technology has quickly become obsolete.
vocabularies.contextSentence: A lot of other technology became obsolete.

vocabularies.definition: A new idea, method, or thing that completely changes the way that something is done.
examples: The invention of the printing press was a game changer.
This new technology is a real game changer for the industry.
vocabularies.contextSentence: because it was a real game changer.

vocabularies.definition: The state of depending on or trusting in something or someone.
examples: Our reliance on fossil fuels is unsustainable.
There is an increasing reliance on technology in education.
vocabularies.contextSentence: Another one that comes to my mind is our reliance on maps

vocabularies.definition: A thing that prevents someone from concentrating on something else.
examples: The noise was a distraction.
Social media can be a major distraction for students.
vocabularies.contextSentence: I find that phones are a distraction.

grammars.title: Present Perfect Tense
grammars.explanation: Used to describe an action that started in the past and continues to the present or has a result in the present.
examples: I have lived here for five years.
She has finished her homework.
grammars.contextSentence: I've always had kind of okay phones, but last year I decided to upgrade

grammars.title: Past Perfect Tense
grammars.explanation: Used to describe an action that was completed before another action in the past.
examples: I had finished the book before the movie came out.
She had already eaten when I arrived.
grammars.contextSentence: I've had several phones, and I've liked some of them, I've always used Samsung.

grammars.title: Use of 'Whether' to introduce alternatives
grammars.explanation: Used to introduce two or more possibilities.
examples: I don't know whether I should go or stay.
She's not sure whether to buy the red dress or the blue one.
grammars.contextSentence: Whether it's work, I'm using my laptop, my phone equipment for my podcast or sports.

grammars.title: Use of 'For example' to introduce examples
grammars.explanation: Used to introduce a specific instance to illustrate a point.
examples: For example, the price of gasoline has increased.
For example, many people enjoy playing sports.
grammars.contextSentence: For example, people rely on modern technology for writing

grammars.title: Use of 'because' to express reason
grammars.explanation: Used to introduce the reason for something.
examples: I am happy because I passed the exam.
She is tired because she worked all day.
grammars.contextSentence: I think the the biggest technological development that comes to my mind is the phone, because it was a real game changer.

grammars.title: Use of 'so' to express result
grammars.explanation: Used to introduce the result or consequence of something.
examples: It was raining, so I took an umbrella.
He was tired, so he went to bed early.
grammars.contextSentence: So I was excited to have this that I can carry around with me day to day.

grammars.title: Use of 'not only...but also'
grammars.explanation: Used to add information and emphasize a point.
examples: She is not only intelligent but also kind.
He not only speaks English but also French.
grammars.structure: Not only + auxiliary verb + subject + ... but also ...
grammars.contextSentence: And it didn't disappoint. I was really impressed.

lesson.notesContent:
# Technology — Ghi chú học tập (Band 6.0)

## Từ vựng quan trọng

### curriculum `/kəˈrɪkjələm/`
**Nghĩa:** The subjects that are taught in a school, college, etc., or the things that are studied in a particular subject.
**Dịch:** chương trình học
**Ví dụ:** *The school has a very broad curriculum.*

### golden period `/ˈɡəʊldən ˈpɪəriəd/`
**Nghĩa:** A time of great success, happiness, or prosperity.
**Dịch:** giai đoạn vàng
**Ví dụ:** *The company is enjoying a golden period of growth.*

### technophile `/ˈtɛknəfaɪl/`
**Nghĩa:** A person who is enthusiastic about new technology.
**Dịch:** người yêu công nghệ
**Ví dụ:** *He is a true technophile, always eager to try the latest gadgets.*

### technophobe `/ˈtɛknəfəʊb/`
**Nghĩa:** A person who dislikes or fears new technology.
**Dịch:** người sợ công nghệ
**Ví dụ:** *My grandmother is a technophobe and struggles with computers.*

### gadget `/ˈɡædʒɪt/`
**Nghĩa:** A small, useful machine or device.
**Dịch:** tiện ích
**Ví dụ:** *I have a lot of gadgets in my kitchen.*

### immediately `/ɪˈmiːdiətli/`
**Nghĩa:** Without any delay; instantly.
**Dịch:** ngay lập tức
**Ví dụ:** *I knew immediately that something was wrong.*

### upgrade `/ʌpˈɡreɪd/`
**Nghĩa:** To improve something or make it more modern.
**Dịch:** nâng cấp
**Ví dụ:** *I decided to upgrade my computer.*

### intuitive `/ɪnˈtjuːɪtɪv/`
**Nghĩa:** Using or based on intuition; readily learned or understood.
**Dịch:** trực quan
**Ví dụ:** *The software has an intuitive interface.*

### videography `/vɪdiˈɒɡrəfi/`
**Nghĩa:** The process or art of making videos.
**Dịch:** quay phim
**Ví dụ:** *He has a passion for videography.*

### obsolete `/ˈɒbsəliːt/`
**Nghĩa:** No longer produced or used; out of date.
**Dịch:** lỗi thời
**Ví dụ:** *Many traditional farming methods have become obsolete.*

### game changer `/ˈɡeɪm ˌtʃeɪndʒər/`
**Nghĩa:** A new idea, method, or thing that completely changes the way that something is done.
**Dịch:** thay đổi cuộc chơi
**Ví dụ:** *The invention of the printing press was a game changer.*

### reliance `/rɪˈlaɪəns/`
**Nghĩa:** The state of depending on or trusting in something or someone.
**Dịch:** sự phụ thuộc
**Ví dụ:** *Our reliance on fossil fuels is unsustainable.*

## Điểm ngữ pháp

### Present Perfect Tense
**Cấu trúc:** `Subject + have/has + past participle`
Used to describe an action that started in the past and continues to the present or has a result in the present.
> I have lived here for five years.

### Past Perfect Tense
**Cấu trúc:** `Subject + had + past participle`
Used to describe an action that was completed before another action in the past.
> I had finished the book before the movie came out.

### Use of 'Whether' to introduce alternatives
**Cấu trúc:** `Whether + clause`
Used to introduce two or more possibilities.
> I don't know whether I should go or stay.

### Use of 'For example' to introduce examples
**Cấu trúc:** `For example, + sentence`
Used to introduce a specific instance to illustrate a point.
> For example, the price of gasoline has increased.

### Use of 'because' to express reason
**Cấu trúc:** `Sentence + because + clause`
Used to introduce the reason for something.
> I am happy because I passed the exam.

### Use of 'so' to express result
**Cấu trúc:** `Sentence + so + clause`
Used to introduce the result or consequence of something.
> It was raining, so I took an umbrella.

lesson.title: Nail Your Opinion Essay in 17 Minutes: IELTS Writing Task 2 (Agree or Disagree)

videos.title: Nail Your Opinion Essay in 17 Minutes: IELTS Writing Task 2 (Agree or Disagree)

vocabularies.definition: difficult in an interesting or enjoyable way
examples: The exam was challenging.
It's a challenging task to learn a new language.
vocabularies.contextSentence: I've selected quite a challenging topic that was used in real exams

vocabularies.definition: used to ask or talk about how much something is true or how great something is
examples: To what extent do you agree with this statement?
To what extent is climate change caused by humans?
vocabularies.contextSentence: to what extent do you agree or disagree so this is an opinion essay

vocabularies.definition: to keep something at the same level or standard
examples: He maintained his composure despite the pressure.
The company is struggling to maintain its market share.
vocabularies.contextSentence: your ability to maintain a good point of view

vocabularies.definition: urgent; needing to be dealt with immediately
examples: The government is facing pressing economic problems.
There are pressing issues that need to be addressed.
vocabularies.contextSentence: that even though there are pressing problems on Earth this is still money well spent

vocabularies.definition: to change the use or purpose of something
examples: The government is diverting funds from education to defense.
The company diverted resources to a new project.
vocabularies.contextSentence: I firmly believe that diverting financial resources from space exploration would be a mistake

vocabularies.definition: an advantage or profit gained from something
examples: The benefits of exercise are well-known.
The company offers a range of benefits to its employees.
vocabularies.contextSentence: these projects have brought and will continue to bring great benefits to humanity

vocabularies.definition: to express the meaning of something using different words, especially to achieve greater clarity
examples: The student was asked to paraphrase the passage.
Paraphrasing can help you understand the text better.
vocabularies.contextSentence: In the first notice the paraphrasing spend too much money on Space research becomes the millions

vocabularies.definition: needing immediate attention
examples: The situation requires urgent action.
The doctor said the patient needed urgent care.
vocabularies.contextSentence: would be better invested in addressing urgent issues on this planet

vocabularies.definition: to make an action or process easier
examples: The new software facilitates communication.
The teacher facilitated a discussion among the students.
vocabularies.contextSentence: have not only facilitated scientific advances in areas such as weather monitoring and navigation

vocabularies.definition: very great or intense
examples: The experience had a profound effect on her.
The philosopher's ideas were profound.
vocabularies.contextSentence: we have witnessed profound changes in the way we connect with each other

vocabularies.definition: connected with each other
examples: The world is becoming increasingly interconnected.
The internet has interconnected people all over the world.
vocabularies.contextSentence: satellite technology has interconnected the world shaping the way we live today

vocabularies.definition: a useful result of an activity designed to produce something else
examples: Many spin-offs have resulted from space exploration.
The company developed a spin-off product from its main line.
vocabularies.contextSentence: these spinoffs cover a wide range from comfortable memory phone mattresses to Tiny cameras in Mobile

vocabularies.definition: to produce or provide something
examples: The research yielded some surprising results.
The investment is expected to yield a good return.
vocabularies.contextSentence: how Innovations originally intended for space exploration continue to yield practical benefits

vocabularies.definition: the action of spending funds
examples: Government expenditure on healthcare has increased.
The company's expenditure was higher than expected.
vocabularies.contextSentence: there is no doubt that the expenditure on Space research is enormous

grammars.title: To what extent questions
grammars.explanation: Understanding the structure and requirements of 'To what extent do you agree/disagree' essay questions.
examples: To what extent do you agree with the statement?
To what extent is this a problem?
grammars.structure: Question: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
grammars.contextSentence: to what extent do you agree or disagree so this is an opinion essay

grammars.title: Complex Sentences with 'Although'
grammars.explanation: Using 'although' to create complex sentences and show contrast.
examples: Although it was raining, we went for a walk.
Although she was tired, she kept working.
grammars.contextSentence: Although there are undoubtedly many problems on Earth that need tackling I firmly believe that diverting financial resources from space exploration would be a mistake

grammars.title: Present Perfect Tense
grammars.explanation: Using the present perfect tense to show the ongoing effects of past actions.
examples: I have lived here for five years.
She has finished her homework.
grammars.contextSentence: to show how developments which began some time ago continue to have effect on us today

grammars.title: Using Present Participles
grammars.explanation: Using present participles (-ing) to combine sentences and add detail.
examples: She walked to the store, buying some milk.
He studied hard, passing the exam.
grammars.contextSentence: because of satellites we have seen big changes in how we connect with each other comma using the internet

grammars.title: Not only... but also...
grammars.explanation: Using 'not only... but also...' to present two related ideas.
examples: Not only is he intelligent, but he is also kind.
She is not only a talented singer, but also a great dancer.
grammars.structure: Not only + auxiliary verb + subject + but also...
grammars.contextSentence: satellites orbiting the Earth have not only facilitated scientific advances but have also transformed our daily lives

grammars.title: Referencing
grammars.explanation: Using pronouns and demonstratives to refer back to previous ideas.
examples: This is a good example.
These are the results.
grammars.contextSentence: be careful to use referencing in your essay because examiners check if you do now

grammars.title: Linking words
grammars.explanation: Using linking words to connect ideas between sentences and paragraphs.
examples: However, the results were different.
Furthermore, the study showed...
grammars.contextSentence: Furthermore carton AG Technologies initially developed for space exploration have been successfully adapted and turned into every typ products we use on Earth

lesson.notesContent:
# Technology — Ghi chú học tập (Band 6.0)

## Từ vựng quan trọng

### challenging `/ˈtʃælɪndʒɪŋ/`
**Nghĩa:** difficult in an interesting or enjoyable way
**Dịch:** thử thách
**Ví dụ:** *The exam was challenging.*

### to what extent ` /tuː wɒt ɪkˈstɛnt/`
**Nghĩa:** used to ask or talk about how much something is true or how great something is
**Dịch:** ở mức độ nào
**Ví dụ:** *To what extent do you agree with this statement?*

### maintain `/meɪnˈteɪn/`
**Nghĩa:** to keep something at the same level or standard
**Dịch:** duy trì
**Ví dụ:** *He maintained his composure despite the pressure.*

### pressing `/ˈpresɪŋ/`
**Nghĩa:** urgent; needing to be dealt with immediately
**Dịch:** cấp bách
**Ví dụ:** *The government is facing pressing economic problems.*

### diverting `/daɪˈvɜːrtɪŋ/`
**Nghĩa:** to change the use or purpose of something
**Dịch:** chuyển hướng
**Ví dụ:** *The government is diverting funds from education to defense.*

### benefits `/ˈbenɪfɪts/`
**Nghĩa:** an advantage or profit gained from something
**Dịch:** lợi ích
**Ví dụ:** *The benefits of exercise are well-known.*

### paraphrasing `/ˈpærəfreɪzɪŋ/`
**Nghĩa:** to express the meaning of something using different words, especially to achieve greater clarity
**Dịch:** diễn giải
**Ví dụ:** *The student was asked to paraphrase the passage.*

### urgent `/ˈɜːrdʒənt/`
**Nghĩa:** needing immediate attention
**Dịch:** khẩn cấp
**Ví dụ:** *The situation requires urgent action.*

### facilitated `/fəˈsɪlɪteɪtɪd/`
**Nghĩa:** to make an action or process easier
**Dịch:** tạo điều kiện
**Ví dụ:** *The new software facilitates communication.*

### profound `/prəˈfaʊnd/`
**Nghĩa:** very great or intense
**Dịch:** sâu sắc
**Ví dụ:** *The experience had a profound effect on her.*

### interconnected `/ˌɪntərkəˈnektɪd/`
**Nghĩa:** connected with each other
**Dịch:** liên kết
**Ví dụ:** *The world is becoming increasingly interconnected.*

### spin-offs `/ˈspɪnɒfs/`
**Nghĩa:** a useful result of an activity designed to produce something else
**Dịch:** sản phẩm phụ
**Ví dụ:** *Many spin-offs have resulted from space exploration.*

## Điểm ngữ pháp

### To what extent questions
**Cấu trúc:** `Question: To what extent do you agree or disagree?`
Understanding the structure and requirements of 'To what extent do you agree/disagree' essay questions.
> To what extent do you agree with the statement?

### Complex Sentences with 'Although'
**Cấu trúc:** `Although + clause, clause.`
Using 'although' to create complex sentences and show contrast.
> Although it was raining, we went for a walk.

### Present Perfect Tense
**Cấu trúc:** `Subject + have/has + past participle`
Using the present perfect tense to show the ongoing effects of past actions.
> I have lived here for five years.

### Using Present Participles
**Cấu trúc:** `Clause, + V-ing + ...`
Using present participles (-ing) to combine sentences and add detail.
> She walked to the store, buying some milk.

### Not only... but also...
**Cấu trúc:** `Not only + auxiliary verb + subject + but also...`
Using 'not only... but also...' to present two related ideas.
> Not only is he intelligent, but he is also kind.

### Referencing
**Cấu trúc:** `Pronoun/demonstrative + noun`
Using pronouns and demonstratives to refer back to previous ideas.
> This is a good example.

cards.backContent:
Nghĩa: thử thách

Phát âm: /ˈtʃælɪndʒɪŋ/

Ví dụ: The exam was challenging.

Đồng nghĩa: becall, call out

Nghĩa: ở mức độ nào

Phát âm: /tuː wɒt ɪkˈstɛnt/

Ví dụ: To what extent do you agree with this statement?

Nghĩa: duy trì

Phát âm: /meɪnˈteɪn/

Ví dụ: He maintained his composure despite the pressure.

Nghĩa: cấp bách

Phát âm: /ˈpresɪŋ/

Ví dụ: The government is facing pressing economic problems.

Đồng nghĩa: depress, hit, strike, emphasize

Nghĩa: chuyển hướng

Phát âm: /daɪˈvɜːrtɪŋ/

Ví dụ: The government is diverting funds from education to defense.

Đồng nghĩa: offlead

Nghĩa: lợi ích

Phát âm: /ˈbenɪfɪts/

Ví dụ: The benefits of exercise are well-known.

Đồng nghĩa: advantage, aid, assistance, boon

Nghĩa: diễn giải

Phát âm: /ˈpærəfreɪzɪŋ/

Ví dụ: The student was asked to paraphrase the passage.

Nghĩa: khẩn cấp

Phát âm: /ˈɜːrdʒənt/

Ví dụ: The situation requires urgent action.

Đồng nghĩa: needly, pressing

Nghĩa: tạo điều kiện

Phát âm: /fəˈsɪlɪteɪtɪd/

Ví dụ: The new software facilitates communication.

Đồng nghĩa: ease

Nghĩa: sâu sắc

Phát âm: /prəˈfaʊnd/

Ví dụ: The experience had a profound effect on her.

Nghĩa: liên kết

Phát âm: /ˌɪntərkəˈnektɪd/

Ví dụ: The world is becoming increasingly interconnected.

Đồng nghĩa: anastomose

Nghĩa: sản phẩm phụ

Phát âm: /ˈspɪnɒfs/

Ví dụ: Many spin-offs have resulted from space exploration.

Đồng nghĩa: derivate, descendant, hive-off

Nghĩa: mang lại

Phát âm: /jiːld/

Ví dụ: The research yielded some surprising results.

Đồng nghĩa: bear, capitulate, cede, defer

Nghĩa: chi tiêu

Phát âm: /ɪkˈspendɪtʃər/

Ví dụ: Government expenditure on healthcare has increased.

Đồng nghĩa: charge, cost, disbursement, expense
